description: indxr accuracy benchmark v2: agent-loop comparison on indxr's own codebase

questions.question: I need to change how tokens are estimated. Where is that logic?
questions.ground_truth: src/budget.rs contains the estimate_tokens function

questions.question: I want to modify how MCP tool calls are routed. Where is the main dispatcher that maps tool names to handler functions?
questions.ground_truth: handle_tool_call in src/mcp/tools.rs

questions.question: What's the main data structure that holds the parsed representation of a single source file? Where is it defined?
questions.ground_truth: FileIndex in src/model/mod.rs

questions.question: I need to add a new declaration kind (like 'Hook' for React). Where is DeclKind defined and what are the current variants?
questions.ground_truth: DeclKind is in src/model/declarations.rs. Variants: Function, Struct, Enum, Trait, Impl, Constant, Static, TypeAlias, Module, Class, Field, Variant, Method, Interface, Namespace, etc.

questions.question: I'm refactoring src/mcp/helpers.rs. Which other files import from it so I know the blast radius?
questions.ground_truth: src/mcp/tools.rs and src/mcp/tests.rs import from helpers.rs

questions.question: I want to add a field to the Declaration struct. Which files use it so I can assess the impact?
questions.ground_truth: Files that import/use Declaration include src/mcp/tools.rs, src/mcp/helpers.rs, src/filter.rs, src/budget.rs, src/diff.rs, src/dep_graph.rs

questions.question: What's the high-level project structure? What are the main modules in src/?
questions.ground_truth: cache/, model/, mcp/, output/, parser/, walker/, and standalone files like cli.rs, indexer.rs, budget.rs, filter.rs, diff.rs, watch.rs, workspace.rs, init.rs, etc.

questions.question: I need to trace how a WorkspaceIndex gets created. Which functions build or return one?
questions.ground_truth: build_workspace_index, detect_and_build_workspace, and regenerate_workspace_index in src/indexer.rs

questions.question: I need to add tree-sitter support for a new language. Where are the existing tree-sitter query files?
questions.ground_truth: src/parser/queries/ directory containing rust.rs, python.rs, javascript.rs, typescript.rs, go.rs, java.rs, c.rs, cpp.rs, mod.rs

questions.question: What does the data model look like in src/model/? I need to understand all the core types before I start contributing.
questions.ground_truth: In src/model/mod.rs: CodebaseIndex, FileIndex, Import, TreeEntry, IndexStats, WorkspaceIndex, MemberIndex. In src/model/declarations.rs: Declaration, ComplexityMetrics, Relationship

questions.question: I want to understand what indexer.rs depends on from the rest of the codebase. What does it import?
questions.ground_truth: It imports from crate::cache, crate::model, crate::output, crate::parser, crate::walker, crate::workspace

questions.question: Where are all the enum types in the project? I want to check if I need a new enum or can extend an existing one.
questions.ground_truth: Enums in src/model/declarations.rs (DeclKind, Visibility, RelKind), src/languages.rs (Language), src/cli.rs (Command, OutputFormat, etc.), src/dep_graph.rs, src/workspace.rs, src/error.rs

questions.question: What public API does src/budget.rs expose? I want to know what I can call from other modules.
questions.ground_truth: Two public functions: estimate_tokens(text: &str) -> usize and apply_token_budget(index: &mut CodebaseIndex, max_tokens: usize)

questions.question: I want to add a new config option. What fields does IndexConfig currently have?
questions.ground_truth: root: PathBuf, cache_dir: PathBuf, max_file_size: u64, max_depth: Option<usize>, exclude: Vec<String>, no_gitignore: bool

questions.question: I want to understand what MCP tools are available. How many tool handler functions are there in src/mcp/tools.rs, and what are some of them?
questions.ground_truth: About 23 tool_* functions including tool_lookup_symbol, tool_read_source, tool_get_file_summary, tool_search_relevant, tool_get_callers, tool_explain_symbol, tool_batch_file_summaries, tool_get_public_api, etc.

questions.question: What does FileIndex actually contain? I need to know the struct fields to add a new one.
questions.ground_truth: path: PathBuf, language: Language, size: u64, lines: usize, imports: Vec<Import>, declarations: Vec<Declaration>

questions.question: Which functions reference estimate_tokens? I changed its signature and need to update all call sites.
questions.ground_truth: Referenced in src/budget.rs (where it's defined) and src/mcp/tools.rs (where it's called for token estimation in tool responses)

questions.question: What output formats does indxr support? I want to add a new one.
questions.ground_truth: Markdown, JSON, and YAML

questions.question: How does the token budget pruning work? I need to understand the stages to add a new one. What gets cut first vs last?
questions.ground_truth: Progressive stages: 1) Truncate long doc comments, 2) Strip all doc comments, 3) Remove private declarations, 4) Remove children, 5) Drop least-important files. Ordered from least lossy to most lossy.

questions.question: What happens if I send an unknown tool name to the MCP server? Does it crash or return an error?
questions.ground_truth: Returns a tool_error with message 'Unknown tool: <name>'. Does not crash or panic.

questions.question: I'm writing a client for the read_source tool. What parameters are required and what happens if I pass neither a symbol name nor line range?
questions.ground_truth: path is required. You must provide either 'symbol'/'symbols' or both 'start_line' and 'end_line'. Without either, it returns an error.

questions.question: How does file importance scoring work in the budget system? I want to boost test files — what factors currently affect the score?
questions.ground_truth: Entry point files score highest (main.rs=100, lib.rs=90, mod.rs=50). Depth penalty (depth*5). Public declaration count bonus (count*3). Line count penalty (lines/100).

questions.question: Does the watch mode re-index on every single file save, or does it batch changes? I'm worried about performance with frequent saves.
questions.ground_truth: It coalesces events — after receiving a change, it drains all queued events with try_recv() and re-indexes only once per burst.

questions.question: How does the cache decide if a file needs re-parsing? I'm seeing stale results after editing files.
questions.ground_truth: Cache checks mtime (modification time) and file size. If either changed, the cached entry is considered invalid and the file gets re-parsed.

questions.question: How does estimate_tokens work? What's the conversion formula from text to token count?
questions.ground_truth: Divides byte length by 4 using ceiling division: text.len().div_ceil(4). Roughly 4 characters per token.

questions.question: What does batch_file_summaries return when there are hundreds of matching files? Does it return all of them?
questions.ground_truth: No, it caps results at 30 files. Returns the count, total_matched (actual number), and the first 30 summaries.

questions.question: If the current token count is already within the budget, does apply_token_budget still strip doc comments and private declarations?
questions.ground_truth: No, it returns immediately without any truncation.

questions.question: If I request a symbol via read_source but also pass start_line, which takes priority?
questions.ground_truth: The symbol parameter takes priority. The code checks for symbol first in an if-else chain.

questions.question: What does get_file_summary return? I need to know the JSON structure to parse it in my client.
questions.ground_truth: Returns: file (path), language, size, lines, imports (array), declarations (shallow), counts (kind -> count map), has_tests (bool), public_symbols (count)

questions.question: What error does read_source give when the requested symbol doesn't exist in the file?
questions.ground_truth: Returns 'Symbol <name> not found in <path>'

questions.question: What happens if a function is really long (say 500 lines) and I request it via read_source? Does it dump the whole thing?
questions.ground_truth: No, it truncates to 200 lines for single-symbol mode.

questions.question: How does the MCP server format error responses? I'm seeing isError in the JSON — how does tool_error structure its output?
questions.ground_truth: tool_error returns a JSON Value with isError: true and a text content block containing the error message string.

questions.question: What caching strategy does indxr use? I want to understand the fast path vs the full content check.
questions.ground_truth: Uses mtime+size as a fast metadata check (avoids reading file content). Content hash (xxh3) is computed at insert time but not checked on reads — the metadata check is sufficient.

questions.question: Why is the MCP tool dispatcher split? regenerate_index and get_diff_summary are handled separately from handle_tool_call. What do they need that other tools don't?
questions.ground_truth: regenerate_index needs mutable access to WorkspaceIndex and WorkspaceConfig. get_diff_summary needs ParserRegistry. The generic handle_tool_call only takes &WorkspaceIndex (immutable).

questions.question: Why does the cache use both mtime+size checks AND xxh3 content hashing? Seems redundant — why not just use one?
questions.ground_truth: mtime+size is a fast metadata-only check that avoids reading file content from disk. The hash is computed at insert time for accuracy. This two-tier approach gives fast cache lookups while maintaining correctness.

questions.question: Why is there a 200-line cap on read_source? Seems annoying when I want to read a big function.
questions.ground_truth: To prevent dumping large amounts of code into the LLM context, wasting tokens. Forces targeted reading.

questions.question: In the budget system, why are doc comments stripped before private declarations? I'd have thought removing private stuff saves more tokens.
questions.ground_truth: Doc comments are the least lossy thing to remove — they don't affect structural information. Private declarations are actual code structure. The stages go from least lossy to most lossy.

questions.question: In the budget system, why are deeper files scored as less important? Couldn't a deeply nested file be critical?
questions.ground_truth: Files near the project root tend to be architecturally significant (entry points, module definitions). Deeper files are typically implementation details or utilities.

questions.question: In the watch module, the output path is canonicalized AFTER the first write, not before. Why? The comment in the code explains this.
questions.ground_truth: The file must exist before it can be canonicalized. And the canonical path is needed to filter out self-triggered re-indexes when the output file changes.

questions.question: In Stage 5 of apply_token_budget, why does the loop keep at least one file even when over budget? Why not drop everything?
questions.ground_truth: To ensure the output is never completely empty. At least the most important file is preserved so there's some useful structural info.

questions.question: Why does handle_tool_call take &WorkspaceIndex (immutable reference) rather than &mut? What's the safety benefit?
questions.ground_truth: Immutable reference guarantees tool calls can't corrupt the index state. Only regenerate_index (which rebuilds the index) needs mutation.

questions.question: In apply_token_budget, files are sorted by file_importance in ascending order before Stage 5 drops them. Why ascending instead of descending?
questions.ground_truth: Files are dropped from the front (index 0). Ascending sort puts least important files first, so they get dropped first. Most important files survive.

questions.question: Why does batch_file_summaries cap at 30 files? What goes wrong if it returns more?
questions.ground_truth: Each summary includes metadata, imports, and declarations. 30+ summaries would create a huge response that wastes tokens and is hard for the LLM to parse.
